title: Intense Turkish Eggplant Dolma
description: An intense, roasted Turkish eggplant dolma packed with aromatic sazon, fresh herbs, and cauliflower rice for a paleo-friendly twist.
instructions: Slice each eggplant lengthwise into 1/2 inch thick strips, sprinkle with salt, and let sit for 20 minutes to draw out bitterness.
Rinse the strips, pat them dry, brush with olive oil, and roast on a sheet for 15 minutes until softened.
While the eggplants roast, sauté the onion and garlic in a pan until fragrant.
Add the cauliflower rice, tomatoes, parsley, mint, lemon juice, sazon, salt, and pepper to the pan and cook for 5 minutes until the rice is tender.
Remove the pan from heat, allow the mixture to cool slightly, then spread it evenly onto each roasted eggplant slice.
Roll the stuffed slices up, place them seam side down in a greased baking dish, and bake for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve the dolmas hot, garnished with extra herbs if desired.
